name: Kala KA-C Concert Ukulele
introText: The Kala KA-C is the most recommended concert ukulele under $100 — mahogany top, back and sides, Aquila Nylgut strings, and a 38cm scale length that's comfortable for adult players transitioning from guitar or starting fresh.
whatWorks: Concert size (vs. soprano) provides more fret access and wider spacing for adult hands — concert ukuleles have a longer scale length and wider neck than soprano ukes, making chord shapes easier for players with average or larger adult hands. Most guitar players transitioning to ukulele prefer concert or tenor size.

Aquila Nylgut strings are the industry-standard premium uke strings — the KA-C ships with Aquila Nylgut, which most uke players purchase and install separately on budget ukuleles that come with inferior plastic strings. Getting them factory-installed at $99 is a value advantage.

Mahogany top, back, and sides produce warm, balanced uke tone — the all-mahogany construction gives the KA-C a warm tonal character with good sustain. It sounds full and resonant for a beginner instrument.

Kala brand is the most trusted in the ukulele segment — Kala ukuleles are played and recommended by professional ukulele players and teachers. The brand credibility means better resale value, better community support, and confidence in quality control.
worthKnowing: Ukuleles require significant tuning when new — new Nylgut strings take several days to break in and will go out of tune frequently for the first week of playing. This is normal for nylon-type strings and not a defect. Tune frequently at first.
Concert is not the smallest size — if you're buying for a child under age 8, consider a soprano ukulele. For teenagers and adults, concert is the right choice.
No tuner or bag included at the base price — a clip-on tuner ($15-20 CAD) is essential for keeping any ukulele in tune. The KA-C doesn't include one.
Not electro-acoustic — the KA-C has no built-in pickup. For performing at open mics or amplified settings, a separate clip-on pickup is needed or the KA-CG (with pickup) is the appropriate upgrade.

commonComplaints.title: New strings require extensive break-in period…
commonComplaints.body: New strings require extensive break-in period — Nylgut strings go out of tune constantly for the first few days of playing as they stretch. Some players mistake this for a defective instrument. Tune frequently and they stabilize.

commonComplaints.title: No clip-on tuner included…
commonComplaints.body: No clip-on tuner included — a digital clip-on tuner is essential for a beginner ukulele but must be purchased separately. Should be bundled at this price.

commonComplaints.title: No bag or case for transport protection…
commonComplaints.body: No bag or case for transport — the KA-C ships in a box without a protective bag or gig bag for transport and storage. A basic gig bag ($20-30) is a recommended add-on purchase.

verdict: The Kala KA-C is the right first concert ukulele. Factory Aquila Nylgut strings, all-mahogany construction, comfortable concert neck dimensions, and Kala's market-leading quality control make it the default recommendation from ukulele teachers and communities. Budget $20-30 more for a clip-on tuner and gig bag and you have everything you need to start.

realBuyers.quote: Guitar player of 10 years. The concert size felt natural immediately — fret spacing and chord shapes translated well from guitar. Love the warmth of the mahogany tone.
My daughter's ukulele teacher specifically recommended Kala as the brand to start with. Picked up the KA-C and she loves it.
The Aquila strings are the key thing. Other brands at this price come with horrible plastic strings. Kala ships Aquila and it makes a massive sound difference.

metaDescription: Kala KA-C concert ukulele review for Canada: mahogany, Aquila Nylgut strings, 15 frets, 38cm scale. ~$99 CAD on Amazon.ca. Best beginner concert ukulele.
